back_translation: In those days, a great crowd had gathered together once again, and they had nothing to eat. He royally called His disciples to come, and royally said to them,
"We have compassion-pity on this crowd, because they have stayed with Us for three days now and have nothing to eat.
If We send them back home while they are still hungry, they will collapse exhausted along the way; and some of them have come from afar besides."
His disciples respectfully replied, "In a desolate wilderness like this, who could find bread to feed all these people until they are full?"
He royally asked them, "How many loaves of bread do you have?" They respectfully said, "Seven loaves."

He royally commanded the crowd to sit down on the ground, then royally took the seven loaves, gave thanks to God, royally broke them, and royally gave them to His disciples to distribute; and they distributed them to the crowd.
They also had a few small fish; He royally blessed those fish, then royally ordered that those fish be distributed as well.
All the people ate until they were filled and satisfied, and the disciples gathered up the leftover fragments — seven large baskets full.
There were about four thousand people, and He royally sent them back.
Immediately He royally embarked on the boat with His disciples and royally went to the region of Dalmanutha.
The Pharisees came out to Him and began to argue with Him, respectfully demanding from Him a sign from the heavens, in order to test Him.
He royally sighed deeply in His royal-spirit, and royally said, "Why does this generation seek a sign? We are telling you the truth, no sign whatsoever will be given to this generation at all."
He royally left them, then royally embarked on the boat once again, and crossed over to the other shore.
The disciples had forgotten to bring bread along, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
He royally charged them, "Watch out, beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and the leaven of Herod."
They discussed and deliberated among themselves, saying that it was because they had no bread.
Jesus royally knew the matter, so He royally said to them, "Why are you debating about not having bread? Do you still not understand, still not perceive? Are your hearts hardened to such an extent?
‹You have eyes, yet do you not see? You have ears, yet do you not hear?› Do you still not remember?
When We broke the five loaves of bread for the five thousand people, how many baskets full of leftovers did you collect?" They respectfully said, "Twelve baskets."
"And when We broke the seven loaves of bread for the four thousand people, how many large baskets full of leftovers did you collect?" They respectfully said, "Seven large baskets."
He royally said to them, "Do you still not understand?"
He royally arrived at the town of Bethsaida; some people brought a blind man to Him, and begged Him to royally touch the man.

He royally took hold of the blind man's hand, then royally led him out of the village. He royally spit upon his eyes, royally laid His royal-hands upon him, and royally asked him, "Do you see anything at all?"
He raised his face to look up and said, "I see people, but they look like trees that are walking around."
He royally laid His royal-hands upon his eyes once again; then he gazed intently, his sight returned to its original state, and he could see everything clearly.
He royally sent him back to his home and royally said, "Do not enter that village at all."
Jesus and His disciples royally departed to the villages in the region of Caesarea Philippi. Along the way, He royally asked His disciples, "Who do people say that We are?"
They respectfully said to Him, "Some say You are John the Baptizer, some say Elijah, and some say You are one of the prophets."
He royally asked them, "What about you all? Who do you say that We are?" Peter respectfully answered Him, "You are the Christ."
He royally charged them not to tell anyone about Him.

He royally began to teach them that the Son of Man must endure many sufferings, must be rejected by the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, must be put to death, and after three days have passed, He will royally rise up anew.
He royally spoke this matter openly. So Peter pulled Him aside to talk one-on-one and began to respectfully object to Him.
He royally turned around, royally beheld His disciples, then royally rebuked Peter, saying, "You, get back behind Us, Satan! For you are not thinking the things of God, but the things of men."
He royally called the crowd together with His disciples to come, and royally said to them, "If anyone desires to follow Us, that one must deny himself, take up his own cross, and follow Us.
For whoever wants to preserve his own life will lose his life; but whoever loses his own life for the sake of Us and for the sake of the gospel-good-news, that one will preserve his own life.
What benefit will a person gain, if he gains the whole world but must lose his own life?
Or what can a person bring to exchange for his own life?

For whoever is ashamed of Us and of Our words, in this generation that commits adultery and is sinful — the Son of Man will likewise be ashamed of that one, when He royally comes in the royal-glory of His royal-Father, together with the holy heavenly-messengers."
